mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window despite rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production index values signal immediate economic momentum and capacity utilization improvements, generating positive short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day forward returns in early-cycle periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index values above 210 indicate persistent inflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins and triggers rate hike expectations, causing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 210 correlate with positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates indicate robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease predicts negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC below -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real gross domestic product values signal economic contraction and reduced aggregate demand, which lowers corporate profit expectations and drives negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 decline coincides with positive 60-day forward returns during recessionary periods over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 110 indicates severe supply-side contraction and factory idle capacity, triggering immediate negative market sentiment and short-term equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 110 yield positive 5-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances.
